evidence.note: 500ms absolute ceiling not met for this 26B model — baseline prefill alone is ~1865ms. Fast-path adds only 2.7% overhead (within the 1.50x ratio threshold). 500ms ceiling assumes smaller models or different reference hardware.
evidence.hardware: MacBook Pro (Mac16,5), Apple M4 Max, 128 GB RAM, Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-JANG_4M (4-bit quantization)

VAL-FAST-002.title: Fast-path does not create AsyncStream, StreamingDetokenizer, or ToolCallProcessor

evidence.codePathTrace: Route handler guard (!isStream, maxTokens==1, echo, logprobs) → engine.loglikelihood() → _performLoglikelihood() → ctx.model() → logSoftmax → token indexing → LoglikelihoodResult → JSON response
evidence.negativeTest: Normal streaming request returns SSE chunks confirming streaming pipeline is used for non-fast-path requests

evidence.crossPathComparison: 3 prompts compared fast-path (max_tokens=1) vs normal echo-path (max_tokens=2) — all prompt token logprobs identical (max abs diff = 0.0, max rel diff = 0.0)
evidence.determinismCheck: 5 consecutive runs with same prompt produced identical token_logprobs
evidence.sanityChecks: All logprobs negative, finite, position 0 null, token/logprob counts match

hardware: MacBook Pro (Mac16,5), Apple M4 Max, 128 GB RAM
